verses.text: But God remembered Noah — and every living thing in the ark. To remember in Scripture isn't to recall after forgetting; it means to act on a promise. God turns from judgment toward rescue.
The fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven are closed. The waters retreat slowly — Genesis takes its time describing the receding. Restoration takes longer than destruction.
The fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven are closed. The waters retreat slowly — Genesis takes its time describing the receding. Restoration takes longer than destruction.
On the seventeenth day of the seventh month, the ark settles on the mountains of Ararat. Real geography, real date — Genesis is not vague.
Two and a half months later, the mountain tops become visible. The world is coming back, slowly.
After forty more days, Noah opens the window. He sends out a raven, which keeps flying back and forth. The raven, a scavenger, finds food on floating carcasses and doesn't return.
After forty more days, Noah opens the window. He sends out a raven, which keeps flying back and forth. The raven, a scavenger, finds food on floating carcasses and doesn't return.
Noah sends a dove next, a clean bird that won't eat anything dead. The dove finds no resting place and comes back. Noah pulls her in by his hand. The picture is tender.
Noah sends a dove next, a clean bird that won't eat anything dead. The dove finds no resting place and comes back. Noah pulls her in by his hand. The picture is tender.
Noah waits seven more days and sends the dove again. She returns at evening with an olive leaf in her beak. Life is sprouting on the earth. The olive branch becomes a worldwide symbol of peace.
Noah waits seven more days and sends the dove again. She returns at evening with an olive leaf in her beak. Life is sprouting on the earth. The olive branch becomes a worldwide symbol of peace.
After seven more days Noah sends the dove a third time, and this time she doesn't return. She has found her home.
Noah was 601. On New Year's Day of his new year, the waters dried up. Two months later the earth was fully dry. A new beginning is dated, named, finished.
Noah was 601. On New Year's Day of his new year, the waters dried up. Two months later the earth was fully dry. A new beginning is dated, named, finished.
God speaks to Noah for the first time since the flood began. Come out — you and your family and every creature with you. Be fruitful and multiply. The blessing from Genesis 1 is renewed.
God speaks to Noah for the first time since the flood began. Come out — you and your family and every creature with you. Be fruitful and multiply. The blessing from Genesis 1 is renewed.
God speaks to Noah for the first time since the flood began. Come out — you and your family and every creature with you. Be fruitful and multiply. The blessing from Genesis 1 is renewed.
Noah and his family and every animal come out of the ark — kind by kind, just as they had entered. The world begins again with the same order God had built into it.
Noah and his family and every animal come out of the ark — kind by kind, just as they had entered. The world begins again with the same order God had built into it.
The very first thing Noah does is build an altar and offer worship. Before farming, before planting, before resting — he gives thanks. Worship comes first.

The sweet savour of Noah's offering reaches God. And God makes a promise in His own heart: I will not again curse the ground for man's sake. Even though humanity's inclination is still evil, God will be patient.

While the earth remaineth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat, and summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease. The seasons themselves become a promise — every sunrise is a reminder of grace.
